title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence · Incident Repair Bay
summary: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a layered packet-flow diagnosis and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the responsibilities and failure evidence of all seven OSI reference-model layers.
Map TCP/IP link, internet, transport, and application functions to OSI layers without treating either as packet reality.
Trace data, segments or datagrams, packets, frames, and bits through encapsulation, forwarding, and decapsulation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe network investigation headed "# Evidence: net-packet-capture-limits-1e20zz" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Packet Capture Limits into observable performance evidence.
